chunk_summary: Standardized clinical vignettes for obesity and adolescent safety counselling focus on non-stigmatizing language, family-based care, and structured interventions using SMART metrics, safety screening, and follow-up plans.
learning_objectives: Practice non-stigmatizing language in obesity counselling.
Apply the 5A's framework to identify high-yield behavioural targets.
Screen for safety concerns such as bullying, sleep debt, and eating disorders.
Develop family-based care plans with SMART goals and routine anchors.
key_facts: Case 4: Target sugar-sweetened beverages, replace with water/unsweetened milk/chaas; use SMART metric (sweet drinks/week).
Case 5: Screen for restriction, purging, laxatives, compulsive exercise; escalate care if vitals indicate instability.
Case 6: Address binge–restrict cycles with regular meals, sleep routines, and depression screening.
Follow-up intervals: Arrange review in 2–4 weeks for obesity-related interventions.
Document key elements: family-based plan, stigma screen, routine anchor, and follow-up date.
reference_quote: Motivational Interviewing emphasizes collaborative, non-judgmental communication to support behavior change. (Source: Miller & Rollnick, Motivational Interviewing, 4th Edition)
document_key_topics: Obesity counselling in children and adolescents
Safety screening for eating disorders and bullying
Family-based care and SMART goal setting
